## [00:00 - 03:03]

Hi, this is Don Crosley. Let's talk about the introduction to your webinar. So this is stage one of your webinar, the introduction. All right. So for each section, I am going to show you the recommended time for it. So if you're doing a 60-minute webinar, this is about a two minute. If it's 90 minutes, it's also two minutes. You don't want to spend a lot of time doing the introduction. So the purpose of the introduction is to pre-stage the cell, setting you up as the expert that they should be paying attention to, which helps you to achieve part of the mindset that they need to be able to buy. And you're also wanting to create emotional connection here. So notice, as I say this, nowhere as I talked about the introduction, did I talk about how this introduction is telling them to put away their cell phones, did not read their email. We're going to skip that. As we talked about in the pre-sell, that's sort of ridiculous. What we are going to do is that we are going to start off by creating that emotional and logical connection with you to start this process of getting them to the mindset that you need. This introduction should be short and sweet. You've got two minutes and it's designed to set that tone for the entire webinar. And so you're only using the resume items that give you real credibility and help you to pre-sell your offer and that create an emotional bond with this particular audience. So one of the things I like to do is I like to show a picture of me with my children and grandchildren because it's important to me. It's part of who I am. It's part of my branding and it immediately connects with a lot of people. For me, it's important to have at least one emotional connection during that introduction. Now, there is an optional introductory section that can happen and that is your host introduction. So if you are doing this webinar for someone else's list and they've invited you to come in and do a JV webinar for their list, usually it shouldn't be you starting the webinar. It should be them, the host who is welcoming people to webinar, doing the pre-show and doing the introduction. And so this is another two-minute project whether you're doing a 60 or a 90 minute and it actually comes before the introduction that you do. So what I just told you about, all that stuff, this happens before that. The purpose of this is again to pre-stage that cell, to tell them how they know you and most importantly to give their personal recommendation of you, your content in this webinar and most importantly the product that you're going to be selling. So if they were to say and I know about the product that he's going to be introducing you to you in

## [03:03 - 04:34]

this webinar and I really believe it, you should buy this, that alone will probably increase your cells by 10 to 20 percent. To have the expert who they admire, that person who brought them into this webinar say, this person is going to tell you about a product at the end of this webinar. I believe you should buy it, is huge and will significantly change your results in terms of the cells you'll get out of the webinar. So keys to success in the host introduction, again it needs to be very brief and focus on reminding, on recommending you rather than introducing the material that's in the webinar. That's your job, you're going to do the introduction in just a moment. Their job is to position you with their audience and it's important that you help your host recognize that the better job they do of recommending you in this, the more money they'll make out of the process. Many of your hosts are going to be reluctant to say that. This is the reason why you need to have this conversation well before the webinar starts, so you can work this through with them. I actually recommend you have a call with them the day before where you talk through the webinar, you show them some of the slides, you show them what the product's going to look like, so that they can feel comfortable coming on and saying yes, I do recommend you buy this product.
